title: Rough Idle Diagnostic PID Bundle
description: Targeted live data capture for rough idle complaints. Guides ProcedureEngineer through the most valuable PIDs to read and what each reading means for root cause isolation.
common_symptoms: idle quality degrades at operating temp
physical_solution: Capture this PID bundle at idle (cold and warm if possible). Data pattern reveals whether idle issue is: lean (vacuum leak/MAF), rich (injector/purge valve), misfiring (ignition/compression), or IAC-related.

technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Full — all PIDs are Mode $01 standard. Best captured at idle with engine fully warmed.
preconditions: Engine at operating temperature for warm idle reading
Engine cold (ambient temp) for cold idle comparison
A/C OFF, all accessories OFF for baseline

procedure_steps.description: IDLE RPM: Mode $01 PID $0C. Normal idle: 600-900 RPM. Hunting (oscillating): 500-1100 RPM cycling = IAC/IACV issue or vacuum leak. Below 500 RPM stalling = severe lean or IACV failure. Above 1200 RPM idle = stuck-open IACV or throttle body issue.

FUEL TRIMS AT IDLE: Mode $01 PID $06 (STFT B1) and $07 (LTFT B1). Rough idle with STFT >+10% = lean at idle (vacuum leak, MAF reading low). Rough idle with STFT <-10% = rich at idle (stuck purge valve, leaking injector, coolant temp sensor cold-bias).

ENGINE LOAD AT IDLE: Mode $01 PID $04 (calculated engine load). Normal idle: 15-30%. Higher than expected load at idle (>40%) = high parasitic load or restricted exhaust (check for collapsed cat or plugged DPF).

TIMING ADVANCE AT IDLE: Mode $01 PID $0E. Normal idle: 8-20° BTDC. Excessive retard (0-5° at idle) = knock retard from low-octane fuel or carbon deposits. Negative timing at idle = ECU fighting detonation.
MAF RATE AT IDLE: Mode $01 PID $10. Normal idle: 2-5 g/s per liter of displacement. Below spec = MAF reading low or intake restriction. Above spec = MAF reading high or large vacuum leak after MAF.

COOLANT TEMP vs IAT: Mode $01 PID $05 (ECT) and $0F (IAT). If ECT and IAT both at ambient on warm engine = coolant temp sensor failure (causes rich cold-start strategy at all times = rough warm idle).

MISFIRE AT IDLE PATTERN: P0300 at idle with neutral fuel trims = mechanical misfire (compression, spark, injector). Read per-cylinder misfire counts PID $4C-$53. Cylinder-specific counts point directly to affected cylinder. Swap COP coil to confirm.

EVAP PURGE AT IDLE: If STFT goes rich at idle with no other cause, command purge valve CLOSED via actuator test. If idle immediately smooths and STFT normalizes = stuck-open purge valve flooding intake at idle.

Capture all PIDs above and build pattern: lean-rough, rich-rough, or misfiring-rough. Match pattern to most likely root cause. Fix root cause, clear codes, re-capture same PIDs to confirm values in normal range.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: IAC carbon buildup very common on older 2ZR/2AZ — dirty IACV causes hunting idle. Clean with throttle body cleaner. VVT-i cam code alongside P0300 = cam timing issue causing idle misfire.
oem_notes.Honda/Acura: IACV (Idle Air Control Valve) carbon buildup on K-series common. Honda idle hunting = IACV primary suspect. Also check VTEC solenoid screen (clogged = rough at VTEC engagement RPM, not idle).
oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: IACV failure on QR/VQ. Nissan Altima/Sentra: IACV replacement common at 80k+. VQ35DE: upper intake manifold gaskets cause lean idle that appears as rough idle with lean trims.
oem_notes.Ford: EcoBoost: rough idle at cold start from turbo lag and cold oil in turbo bearings — normal. If rough when warm: IMRC (intake manifold runner control) failure on 2.0/3.5T. Check IMRC motor and runner positions.
oem_notes.GM: AFM engines: rough idle at light throttle from active fuel management (normal 4-cylinder mode feel). Disable AFM to confirm if idle concern is just AFM sensation. 3.6L Pentastar (GM): cam follower failures cause rough idle.
oem_notes.BMW: Valvetronic eccentric shaft sensor failure causes rough idle with no codes initially. VANOS solenoid failures cause warm rough idle. Check crankcase ventilation (Valvetronic cover membrane common failure N52/N55).
oem_notes.VW/Audi: EA888: rough warm idle almost always PCV failure (cracked membrane in valve cover). Check for oil in intake manifold. DSG rough at idle = clutch pack wear (different issue — transmission, not engine).
oem_notes.Stellantis: Hemi 5.7: MDS misfire at idle (deactivation cycling). Disable MDS to confirm. 2.4L Tigershark: PCV failure causing rough idle with oil consumption.
oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: Theta II: GDI carbon on intake valves causes cold rough idle that clears when warm. Walnut blast needed. Also: ISG (idle stop-go) system occasionally causes perceived rough restart on stop-start models.
